master.name: Helvetolide
master.description: Helvetolide® is classified as Musky > Berries > White Flowers. Uses in perfumery: Helvetolide is used in all kinds of perfumes (but espacially in fine fragrance because of its price) to bring a heady pear note and an Ambrette Seeds Absolute base note. It can be used as a fixative, with no cheap sub-effect. It is basically used as Musk T® , but brings more volume and tenacity to the fragrance. Notes: Helvetolide® is the first acyclic musk ever used in perfumery. It participated to the opening of a new molecules category. After it, Romandolide® , Nebulone®, Edenolide® and Sylkolide® were synthesized.
master.notes: Imported from RESONANCE 1+1 V2 RES00246 ws26111030.pdf
